title: Snapshot + EvaluationGraph + node data types
analysis: This task builds the core M2 evaluation data structures: typed graph nodes, an EvaluationGraph backed by PersistentMap, and a Snapshot with provenance tracking. The codebase already has all prerequisite types: ValueCellId/ConstraintNodeId/RealizationNodeId in reify-types/identity.rs, PersistentMap in reify-types/persistent.rs, ContentHash in reify-types/hash.rs, CompiledExpr in reify-types/expr.rs, and CompiledGeometryOp/ValueCellKind/TopologyTemplate in reify-compiler. The M1 evaluator (reify-eval/src/lib.rs) uses flat ValueMap; this task adds the graph layer alongside it without disrupting existing evaluation. New files: reify-types/src/provenance.rs (SnapshotProvenance), reify-eval/src/graph.rs (node types + EvaluationGraph), reify-eval/src/snapshot.rs (Snapshot). ValueCellKind lives in reify-compiler and will be referenced from reify-eval (which already depends on reify-compiler). SourceNodeId already exists in identity.rs with struct fields; the task spec's u64 newtype version is aspirational for later milestones, so we skip changing it. ComputeNodeId is also deferred since ComputeNode is not needed for M2.

prerequisites.description: Add VersionId and SnapshotId types to crates/reify-types/src/identity.rs with derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash) and PartialOrd/Ord for VersionId. Re-export from lib.rs.

Create crates/reify-types/src/provenance.rs with SnapshotProvenance enum (Initial, Edit{changed, parent}, Elaboration{parent}, Resolution{scope, resolved, parent}). Add module declaration and re-export in lib.rs.
Create empty crates/reify-eval/src/graph.rs and crates/reify-eval/src/snapshot.rs files with module declarations in lib.rs. Ensure cargo check passes.

steps.description: Write unit tests for VersionId and SnapshotId in identity.rs: construction, equality, copy semantics, Debug format, VersionId ordering (PartialOrd/Ord), and use as HashMap keys.

Implement VersionId(pub u64) and SnapshotId(pub u64) in identity.rs. VersionId derives PartialOrd+Ord. Both derive Clone+Copy+Debug+PartialEq+Eq+Hash. Add Display impls. Re-export from lib.rs. Tests from step-1 should pass.

Write unit tests for SnapshotProvenance in provenance.rs: construct each variant (Initial, Edit with changed set + parent, Elaboration with parent, Resolution with scope + resolved + parent). Test Debug, Clone, PartialEq.

Implement SnapshotProvenance enum in provenance.rs with Clone+Debug+PartialEq derives. Uses HashSet<ValueCellId> for changed/resolved sets and SnapshotId for parent references. Re-export from lib.rs. Tests from step-3 should pass.
Write unit tests for ValueCellNode in graph.rs: construct with id, kind (Param/Let), cell_type, optional default_expr, content_hash. Verify all fields accessible. Test Debug and Clone.

Implement ValueCellNode struct in graph.rs with fields: id (ValueCellId), kind (ValueCellKind from reify-compiler), cell_type (Type), default_expr (Option<CompiledExpr>), content_hash (ContentHash). Derive Clone+Debug.
Write unit tests for ConstraintNodeData and RealizationNodeData in graph.rs: construct each with their respective fields (id, expr/operations, content_hash). Verify field access, Debug, Clone.

Implement ConstraintNodeData (id: ConstraintNodeId, expr: CompiledExpr, content_hash: ContentHash) and RealizationNodeData (id: RealizationNodeId, operations: Vec<CompiledGeometryOp>, content_hash: ContentHash) in graph.rs. Derive Clone+Debug.

Write unit tests for EvaluationGraph in graph.rs: create empty graph, insert nodes into value_cells/constraints/realizations PersistentMaps, get nodes back, verify len/is_empty. Test clone produces independent copy (insert into clone, original unchanged).

Implement EvaluationGraph struct with three PersistentMap fields: value_cells (ValueCellId→ValueCellNode), constraints (ConstraintNodeId→ConstraintNodeData), realizations (RealizationNodeId→RealizationNodeData). Derive Clone+Debug+Default. Tests from step-9 should pass.

Write tests for EvaluationGraph::from_templates: build a TopologyTemplate with params, lets, constraints, and realizations using TopologyTemplateBuilder. Call from_templates and verify all nodes are populated in the correct PersistentMaps with correct ids and content hashes.

Implement EvaluationGraph::from_templates(templates: &[TopologyTemplate]) -> EvaluationGraph. Iterates templates, converts ValueCellDecl→ValueCellNode, CompiledConstraint→ConstraintNodeData, RealizationDecl→RealizationNodeData, inserting each into the appropriate PersistentMap.

Write tests for EvaluationGraph::topology_fingerprint(): same graph structure produces same fingerprint, adding a node changes fingerprint, order-independent (same nodes inserted in different order produce same fingerprint).

Implement EvaluationGraph::topology_fingerprint(&self) -> ContentHash. Collects content hashes from all node maps, sorts for determinism, combines via ContentHash::combine_all. from_templates should also return the fingerprint or store it.

Write tests for Snapshot: construct with id, version, graph, values, topology_fingerprint, provenance. Test Snapshot::from_compiled_module using a CompiledModuleBuilder — verify graph nodes populated, values initialized to (Undef, Undetermined), provenance is Initial, version/snapshot ids start at 0.

Implement Snapshot struct in snapshot.rs with fields: id (SnapshotId), version (VersionId), graph (EvaluationGraph), values (PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)>), topology_fingerprint (ContentHash), provenance (SnapshotProvenance). Implement Snapshot::from_compiled_module(&CompiledModule) -> Snapshot. Derive Clone+Debug.

Write tests for Snapshot O(1) clone and structural sharing: clone a snapshot, modify the clone's values map (insert a new value), verify original snapshot is unchanged (different len, original values intact). This validates PersistentMap structural sharing at the Snapshot level.

Ensure Snapshot derives Clone (leveraging PersistentMap's O(1) clone). No additional implementation needed if step-16 is correct — this step validates the structural sharing guarantee and fixes any issues found.

Write integration test in crates/reify-eval/tests/snapshot_graph.rs: use bracket_compiled_module() fixture to create Snapshot via from_compiled_module. Verify: 6 value_cells (5 params + 1 let for volume, body is realization not value cell), 3 constraints, 1 realization. Verify provenance is Initial. Clone snapshot, modify clone, verify original unchanged.
Fix any issues found in the integration test. Ensure cargo test --workspace passes with all new tests. Wire up pub exports from reify-eval/src/lib.rs for graph and snapshot modules.

design_decisions.rationale: SnapshotProvenance references only types from reify-types (ValueCellId, SnapshotId). Keeping it in reify-types avoids circular dependencies and allows other crates to reference provenance without depending on reify-eval.

design_decisions.decision: Keep ValueCellKind in reify-compiler, reference from reify-eval
design_decisions.rationale: ValueCellKind is already defined in reify-compiler and used throughout. Moving it to reify-types would be cleaner long-term but is out of scope. reify-eval already depends on reify-compiler, so the reference is safe.

design_decisions.decision: Skip SourceNodeId(u64) and ComputeNodeId(u64) newtype additions
design_decisions.rationale: SourceNodeId already exists in identity.rs with struct fields (module, declaration_index). The task description says SourceNode and ComputeNode come in later milestones. Changing SourceNodeId shape would break existing code for no M2 benefit.

design_decisions.decision: Node data types in reify-eval, not reify-types
design_decisions.rationale: ValueCellNode, ConstraintNodeData, RealizationNodeData reference CompiledExpr (reify-types) AND CompiledGeometryOp/ValueCellKind (reify-compiler). They cannot live in reify-types without introducing a reify-compiler dependency. reify-eval depends on both, making it the natural home.

design_decisions.decision: Snapshot values map uses (Value, DeterminacyState) tuple
design_decisions.rationale: Per the task spec, values in the snapshot track both the value and its determinacy state. Using a PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)> keeps it simple and leverages existing structural sharing.

design_decisions.decision: topology_fingerprint computed from sorted content hashes for determinism
design_decisions.rationale: PersistentMap iteration order is not guaranteed. Sorting hashes before combining ensures the fingerprint is deterministic regardless of insertion order, which is critical for cache invalidation.

design_decisions.decision: Initial snapshot values are (Undef, Undetermined) for all value cells
design_decisions.rationale: from_compiled_module creates the graph structure but does NOT evaluate expressions. Evaluation is a separate concern handled by the Engine. Values start as Undef/Undetermined and get populated during evaluation passes.

design_decisions.decision: Provenance uses std::collections::HashSet (not a persistent set)
design_decisions.rationale: The changed/resolved sets in SnapshotProvenance are small and created once per snapshot transition. They don't need structural sharing — regular HashSet is simpler and sufficient.

reuse.how: Used as the backing store for all EvaluationGraph node maps and Snapshot values map. Provides O(1) clone via structural sharing.
Used for topology_fingerprint computation and content_hash fields on all node data types. ContentHash::combine_all for fingerprint aggregation.
Used as keys in EvaluationGraph PersistentMaps and as identifiers in node data types.
CompiledExpr stored in node data types. Value+DeterminacyState in Snapshot values map. Type in ValueCellNode.

reuse.what: TopologyTemplate, ValueCellDecl, CompiledConstraint, RealizationDecl, ValueCellKind, CompiledGeometryOp
reuse.how: Input to EvaluationGraph::from_templates. ValueCellDecl fields mapped to ValueCellNode fields. CompiledConstraint to ConstraintNodeData. RealizationDecl to RealizationNodeData.
Used in tests to construct TopologyTemplate and CompiledModule fixtures for from_templates and from_compiled_module tests.
Used in integration test (step-19) to verify Snapshot creation from a realistic compiled module.

reuse.what: Expression builder helpers (literal, value_ref, gt, lt)
reuse.how: Used to construct CompiledExpr values for test node data types and template builders.
